title: Nursery Rhyme. DCI. Local.
content:
[The following verses are said by Aubrey to have been sung in his time by the girls of Oxfordshire in a sport called Leap Candle, which is now obsolete.]

        The tailor of Bicester,
            He has but one eye;
        He cannot cut a pair of green galagaskins,
            If he were to try.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. DCI. Local."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
